Example of the Month
This Top Secret sealed %%S & b" packet with your newsletter
contains a complete "Swipe <& Deploy" Sample of a tested,
proven, universally applicable marketing item...
^ One month it may be a direct mail piece, another something to use at a website,
another seasonal program, another a complete tele-seminar or webinar campaign, etc. etc.
Our goal is to get as close to handing you a Turn-key, Ready-To-Use
Marketing/Promotional Tool as is possible, given there are literally thousands of different
kinds of businesses making Glazer-Kennedy Insider's Circle™ membership. To anyone who uses
just one of these a year, this at least QUADRUPLES THE VALUE OF MEMBERSHIP, and makes
every month a quick-to-moneymaking-action month.
IMPORTANT 	NOTE: 	These 	are 	intended 	ONLY 	for 	use 	of 	GKIC 	Members.
Further, 	whenever 	possible, 	helpful 	vendors, 	suppliers 	or 	facilitators 	will 	be
provided 	should 	you 	wish 	to 	use 	them.

-- 1 of 9 --

%%•
The ONE Marketing Strategy Every GKIC
M e m b e r 	S h o u l d 	B e 	U s i n g " 	- >
First....a couple of questions:
1. What is the ONE Marketing Strategy used by GKIC that we would NEVER give up?
2. What is the ONE Marketing Strategy that EVERY GKIC Member should be implementing in
their 	business?
3. What is the ONE Marketing Strategy that should NEVER be delivered 'exclusively' online
regardless of what people tell you?
The answer to all three questions above is....A Newsletter
AND 	there 	are 	many 	reasons 	for 	it. A "properly" 	written* 	newsletter 	will:
• 	Develop 	a 	continued 	relationship 	with 	your 	customers/clients/patients
• Reminds them of your products or services you offer
• 	Substantially 	increases 	their 	spending 	with 	you
• Increases REFERRALS
• 	Get's 	read!!!!!
^
NOTE: *To learn all of the "insider Secrets" of writing newsletters and to see WHAT and WHY we do
what we do each month in the No B.S. Marketing Letter pick up a copy of "How To Create Personality
in 	Copy" 	available 	at 	DanKennedy.com/store.
Below is some helpful information about newsletter marketing
Q: Why do newsletters work so well?
A: Newsletters work well because they tend to be read as informational, making them more welcome
when they are received. People also tend to be more receptive to what you have to say in your
newsletter, because newsletters aren't meant to be sales tools. Rather, they are designed to be a
resource. Because people are conditioned to be less resistant to reading information, which is exactly
what a newsletter should be. This is a HUGE marketing advantage.
Q: What are some of the most common ways people use newsletters?

-- 2 of 9 --

A: Below is a list to consider:
• Newsletters should be mailed to all current and past customers, as well as to any prospects
-_ 	that 	have 	requested 	information 	about 	your 	company.
• Newsletters can be an effective part of a lost customer campaign.
• Your salespeople should hand out copies of your newsletter when they make a sales call.
Integrate the newsletter into the sales process. Point out special articles or news items that
your customer will be interested in.
• Place a stack of newsletters at businesses frequented by customers. This can turn into a simple
cross-promotion. One golf coach I know places her newsletter in a chiropractor's waiting room
and hands out the chiropractor's newsletter as part of an information packet she gives her
clients.
• Give out copies of your newsletter at trade shows and exhibition booths.
• Put a stack on the information table at community events. Place copies in the information rack
at your local Chamber of Commerce.
• And of course, post a PDF copy of your newsletter on your company's web site.
This month's example included inside your S&D envelope is a B to B newsletter used by Gold Luxury
Member Mary Beth Yannessa for her company Tridon Industries and is created by Gold Luxury
Member Jim Palmer's company (No Hassle Newsletters).
As you can see, this newsletter has three main components to it:
-"•~ Fun stuff
• 	Interesting 	stuff
• 	Information 	about 	Tridon 	Industries
NOTE: Perhaps if I'm being critical, the one thing I would like to see that's missing is creating what I
call a referral culture. The best way to do this is by acknowledging people in the newsletter who refer.
(See my article in the March No B.S. Marketing Letter how Dr. Brian Berg brilliantly accomplishes this
strategy)
You might find it interesting to read below what Mary Beth says about her experience with her
newsletter:
Tridon Industries has been sending out one of Jim Palmer's No Hassle Newsletters since July 2009. We receive many
compliments from our customers regarding the content Jim provides but the greatest complement of all was a huge new
contract! We recently featured an article about using our Blaze Shield II insulation at the Hershey Hotel & Restaurant.
Somehow an architect in Hawaii received a copy of our newsletter and contacted our offices. After several discussions
we were awarded a $400,000 contract! Three quick points Jim says about customer newsletters. First, make your
newsletter fun and entertaining and not too much about your work. Second, it can take 6-9 months for your newsletter
to show some results. And finally, Jim also says that newsletters get passed around. I can attest that he is right on all
three counts!
Mary Beth Yannessa
President, Tridon Industries, Inc.
Pottstown, PA 19464

incredibly easy for any business, no matter what industry they're in.

is being sent to you courtesy of

An Expert Resource in the
Insulation and Fire Protection
I n d u s t r i e s

the box? Contact the people
inside the Circle™

value your business. Feel free to
pass this newsletter on to friends
and neighbors. Enjoy!
Mark Your Calendar
Rosa Parks Day - Dec. 1
To celebrate the day in 1955 that
Rosa Parks was arrested for
refusing to give up her seat to a
white passenger on a bus in
Montgomery, AL. The day
marked the birth of the modern
U.S. Civil Rights movement.
Pearl Harbor Day - Dec. 7
Approximately 3,000 people died
in the Japanese attack on Pearl
Harbor, Hawaii, in 1941. The
attack catapulted the U.S. into
World War II.
Wright Brothers Day - Dec. 17
Wilbur and Orvillc Wright
documented the first successful
powered and controlled flights of
an airplane in 1903.
November is also Drunk and
Drugged Driving Prevention
Month and National Stress-Free
Family Holiday Month.
Tips for Better Communication
Tridon Receives ABAA Certification
College Sports Center Finds Good Fit
with Spray Foam
The Edward H. Arnold Sports Center at Lebanon
Valley College offers many amenities, including a
lap pool, racquetball and tennis courts, fitness center,
sauna, and a wellness pool. The mission of the center
is to cater to the health and wellness of the students,
faculty, staff and community members in the form
of recreational activities and fitness programs. What
the school wasn't anticipating, though, was a
condensation issue in the building.
Moisture was collecting on the underside of the metal roof above a racquetball
court, damaging the drop ceiling below, as well as the fiberglass insulation. A
solution was needed in order to protect the building from further damage and to
ensure the safety of the occupants.
Tridon to the rescue! Tridon Industries installed 4" of closed-cell spray
polyurethane insulation to approximately 3790 sq. ft. of metal roof decking. In
addition to adding an R-28 insulation value, the spray foam acts as an air barrier and
moisture vapor retarder, ensuring that no moisture vapor infiltrates the building.
And since spray foam will not shrink or settle, the building and its occupants will
stay safe, dry, and protected for years to come.
Tridon Industries installs foam in-place and spray foam insulation, as well as spray-
applied soundproofing and fireproofing in residential and commercial buildings.
The company also installs roof coatings
and building restoration products. Need
someone to think outside the box? Contact
the people inside the Circle™. Tridon
Industries: An expert resource in the
Insulation and Fireproofing industries. For
more information, or to learn about the
company's other Notable Projects, contact
the company at 800-760-8044 or
info(S>tridonindustries.com.
Tridon Industries - Around the Block Newsletter ®2009 - HIC# PA013941

-- 5 of 9 --

Fall Home
Maintenance
Tips
Part 3 of a 3 part series
Fall is good time to start thinking about
the winter ahead. Follow these home
maintenance tips and be prepared when
cold weather sets in.
DECEMBER is a good month to:
• Review family's fire-escape plan. Be
sure to include the whole household. Check
pressure and expiration date on fire
extinguishers. $$ A new extinguisher is $10
to $50, depending on size.
• Look inside bathroom vanities and
kitchen-sink cabinets for moisture and
other signs of leakage. Inspect pipes for
condensation or slow drips. It only takes
five minutes'.
• Check clothes dryer vents and hoses for
lint buildup that can cause a fire. Clean if
necessary. $$ Hire a pro for about $130 for
a thorough cleaning. Or DJYfor $40 with a
brush-and-rod tool like the Lint Eater
(linteater.com).
• End the year with a little extra financial
protection: Pay January's mortgage before
Dec. 31 so that you can deduct more interest
from the year's tax return.
• Insulate! It's not too late to install new or
add to existing insulation. After all, there are
still a few months of cold weather ahead!
DECEMBER is a good month to buy:
• Snowb lowers
• Power tools
Tridon 	Industries 	thanks
you 	for 	your 	patronage
throughout 	the 	year. 	We
look 	forward 	to 	working
with 	you 	in 	the 	future.
Happy 	Holidays!
Stop Seeing Red and Save More
Green
Does the thought of money being flushed down the drain as a result of
high utility bills make you see red? If so, our new Thermal Imaging
Service will have you seeing more green in no time.
In keeping with our Vision Statement of being the leader in our
industry, we are pleased to announce the launch of our new Thermal
Imaging Service, geared toward the residential and commercial
markets.
This thermography service includes a whole-building visual walk
through and infrared inspection of your residential or commercial
property. During the hour-long inspection, we are looking for ways to
help you save money on utility bills while making your building more
energy efficient. With the help of a thermal imaging camera, we will
be able to tell you where air leakage is occurring, for example, and
where insulation may be missing or insufficient. We are also looking
for causes of other common problems, such as drafty rooms, rooms
that are hard to heat or cool, and issues with mold and mildew.
As part of the package, we issue you a comprehensive report,
complete with thermal images of the building that will show — in
detail - what the building needs, what your options are, and how we
can possibly help solve your issues.
Offering a thermal imaging service seemed a natural progression for
Tridon, since we've been installing insulation in residential and
commercial buildings for 12 years. With the increased emphasis on
reducing greenhouse gases through green initiatives, we see our
thermal imaging consultations as a way to offer an additional service
to our existing customers, while allowing new clients to benefit as
well from lower energy bills, increased energy efficiency, and tax
incentives. We're also hoping our new service will give us an edge in
the increasingly competitive marketplace.
We are specifically targeting older residential and commercial
buildings, where the existence and condition of wall insulation is
unknown. "Up until thermal imaging existed, installing insulation in
existing walls was hit or miss," says our president and owner, Mary
Beth Yannessa. "You couldn't always tell what was hidden behind the
walls of a building. Now, with a thermal imaging camera, we can."
Want to lower your utility bill and make your residential or
commercial property more energy efficient? Tridon can help you save
more green! Contact us TODAY for more information about our
Thermal Imaging Service!
Tridon Industries - Around the Block Newsletter ®2009 - HIC# PA013941

-- 6 of 9 --

Four Tips for Better Communication
Are you one of those people who are
smart but have a tough time
convincing colleagues you know
what you're talking about? Are you
prone to drawing a total blank when
talking to your boss? Here are several
strategies for overcoming anxiety
and communicating with confidence:
• Don't let your voice drop. Speak up and speak proudly. Psychological
studies have proved that, when it comes to effective communication, what
matters most is how you say it, not what you say.
• Don't clutter your speech with like and um and uh. Experts say that when
you remove meaningless words and phrases, you will be perceived as
smarter.
• Don't gesture while you're speaking. Moving your amis and hands,
touching your face, smoothing and twirling your hair—all of these gestures
draw attention away from what you are saying, and, consequently, make you
look less confident in what you're saying.
• Don't resort to lying just to have an answer. Even little white lies
eventually lead to the perception that you don't know what you're talking
about. Resist the temptation when you're stumped and simply say, "I don't
have an answer, but I will get back to you in a short time with one." Showing
VYiaV you arc willing to do the research to find the correct information shows
initiative and looks smarter than any little fib you can come up with to cover
your tracks.
About Our Company
Tridon Industries is an expert in the Insulation and Fireproofing
industries.
The Products we Install:
*/ Masonry Foam Insulation
V Spray Polyurethane Foam
v Spray Applied Fireproofing

V NEW! Thermal Imaging Service
Tell a friend! A referral from one of our satisfied clients is the greatest
compliment we can receive, and we have a generous way of saying thank you!
It's called our Referral Rewards Program. Contact our office for details!
Financially
Speaking
Map Out Your Finances
Personal finance can seem murky and tortuous,
and without a good road map it's easy to feel
lost. The current recession makes careful
handling of your money especially sensitive, so
heed these tips as you make financial plans for
the future:
• Invest. If you have extra money after paying
the bills and funding your tax-deferred
retirement plan to the max, think about investing
the remainder rather than spending it. In the
long run, you're likely to find compounding
returns far more rewarding.
• Strengthen your financial relationship with
your spouse. If you're in a stable relationship
with a spouse or partner, consider strategies like
combining more of your assets, opening
investment accounts for retirement purposes or
your kids' college costs, and diversifying your
investment strategies. You don't have to merge
everything - remember that 401(k) and IRA
plans can't be combined - so you'll still have
-some financial autonomy,--
• Save for college. The right strategy to save for
your children's higher education depends on
several factors: your tax bracket, the investment
flexibility that you require, and the amount you
have to save. You may think your kids can apply
for financial aid, but remember that many
colleges take education savings into account
when calculating a family's need for grants or
loans. Explore 520 savings plans, Education
IRAs, or taxable investment accounts.
• Protect yourselves. There's no such thing as
total job security, so financial advisors
recommend an emergency fund. Calculate what
your fixed expenses are. Take a look at the job
market in your field so you can estimate how
long you may be unemployed if you lose your
job, and start making regular deposits into a
dedicated "lost job" account.
y M*e_ wsfczsoN, aj. ei_rf4 zs^ezvea wm As'35zTcof.-4 com

on the finger pointing."

"AROUND THE BLOCK" NEWSLETTER 	HIC#PA013941 	DECEMBER 2009
What Happened To Reasonable Discussion?
TV columnist Tom Shales has a story that may illustrate the decline of civil discourse in public life these days. As he recounts in the Washington Post,
Shales once got a phone call asking him to appear on a new show. The producer asked him a few questions about the subject he'd be discussing,
designed to determine which side of the issue he supported. Shales replied that he could see both sides of the question—there wasn't a clearly defined,
black-or-white solution. That was the end of the conversation, and Shales never appeared on the show. The producer wanted someone who agreed
positively with one side or the other, not somebody who saw positive aspects of both viewpoints. "1 think this is a very real problem that results in a lot
of yelling where there should be an 'exchange of ideas,' " Shales writes.
Why Choose Tridon?
Because we Guarantee Your Satisfaction!
That's a bold claim. Here's how we back it up:
• We'll work with you until all your questions are answered or
all your problems are solved
• We'll provide an in-depth, no B.S. proposal within 72 hours of
our visit
• There's a lot of conflicting info on insulation/fireproofing out
there. We sift through it all and separate fact from fiction
• We'll get back to you no matter what - even if it's to tell you
(gently and honestly) that we're not the right contractor for you
• We specialize in handling unique and unusual situations that a
lot of other contractors won't touch
Quote of the Month
"You con't commond people to do their best;
they can only commond that of themselves."
-Bob Nelson, author, "1001 Ways to Reward Employees"
Tridon 	Receives 	ABAA 	Certification
Tridon Industries is pleased to announce that it is now a Certified
Air Barrier Installer.
The company received its certification through the Air Barrier
Association of America (ABAA), the national voice of the air
barrier industry. As a spray polyurethanc foam installer, Tridon
was eligible to receive training through the organization's SPF
Installer Training Course. During the three-day course, participants
learn about building science, codes and standards as they pertain to
air barriers; testing, audits, and proper documentation and
reporting; and an overview of building enclosure construction.
Participants also receive field training by installing an air barrier
assembly.
According to the ABAA website, the organization's mission is "To
promote the use and benefits of air barrier systems, educate the
public about air barrier systems and develop a professional air
barrier specialty trade and industry dedicated to the installation of
effective air barrier systems in buildings on a nationwide scale"
Through its efforts, the ABAA strives to raise the standard of the
industry and lead it into the future in a progressive and professional
way.
Tridon Industries participated in the training course in order to
supplement the skills and experience the company already has as
spray foam installers. It is an investment that Tridon hopes will
give it an edge in the increasingly competitive marketplace.
